# frogConvert

> Fast, private, in-browser file conversion. Converts 50+ formats entirely on-device using WebAssembly — no uploads, no server, no data leaves your machine.

## What is frogConvert?

frogConvert is a fork of [Convert to it!](https://github.com/p2r3/convert) with a redesigned UI and additions including an MCP server for programmatic AI agent access, a REST API, and Web Workers for non-blocking conversions. It runs FFmpeg, ImageMagick, Pandoc, and other conversion engines as WASM in the browser.

- **Live app**: https://frogconvert.xyz/
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/ogfrench/frogConvert
- **Tech stack**: Bun, Vite, TypeScript, vanilla CSS — no framework

## Documentation

- [AGENTS.md](https://frogconvert.xyz/AGENTS.md): AI agent guide — how to use the MCP server and REST API to convert files programmatically, discover supported formats, and query conversion paths.
- [AGENT_CONTEXT.md](https://frogconvert.xyz/AGENT_CONTEXT.md): Deep technical reference for developers and agents — architecture, handler interface rules, TraversionGraph algorithm, web workers, testing patterns, and critical gotchas.
- [README.md](https://frogconvert.xyz/README.md): Setup, local development, Docker deployment, and contributing guide.

## Key capabilities

- 50+ file formats: images (PNG, JPEG, WebP, AVIF, SVG…), audio (MP3, FLAC, OGG, WAV…), video (MP4, WebM, GIF…), documents (PDF, DOCX, EPUB, Markdown…), archives (ZIP, TAR…), fonts, data formats
- MCP server (`bun run mcp`) exposing `convert_file`, `list_formats`, `find_conversion_path` tools
- REST API (`bun run api`) as an HTTP alternative to MCP
- Graph-based pathfinding (Dijkstra) to find multi-step conversion routes
- 100% client-side — zero data sent to any server
